purpose: Release integrity ledger for PhantomForce decisions that must not disappear, regress, or be resurrected by stale worktrees.

rules.title: Revenue workflows persist by tenant and never invent contacts
rules.ownerDecision: CRM discovery must use a verified public-research provider or add zero contacts. Manual contacts, proposal drafts, approval records, and Managed Growth reporting persist in the active organization; draft and approval state never implies an external send or execution.

requiredPatterns.description: Unconnected CRM discovery returns an explicit blocked state instead of invented leads.
Visible proposal drafts use the tenant-scoped proposal service.
Visible approval cards use tenant-scoped server persistence.
Analytics mounts the source-backed Managed Growth report.
A complete release gate covers critical UI, database auth, tenant isolation, and Easy CRM.

forbiddenPatterns.description: The server must never fabricate discoverable-looking CRM prospects.
The browser must never synthesize contacts from a natural-language lead request.

rules.title: Live admin must serve the clean deployment checkout
rules.ownerDecision: Accepted admin work only counts as live after it is pushed to origin/main and the local admin server serves the clean phantomforce-live deployment checkout, not a development or stale sibling worktree.

requiredPatterns.description: AGENTS documents the canonical editing checkout.
AGENTS documents the dedicated clean deployment checkout.
The live-source doctor checks the running admin root.

requiredPatterns.pattern: Resurrection guard[\s\S]*Dashboard repository environment override is empty or canonical[\s\S]*Recurring admin sync launches the tracked canonical hidden runner[\s\S]*Legacy login fallback watcher is absent or canonical[\s\S]*No stale admin watcher process can reclaim the live ports
requiredPatterns.description: The strict doctor rejects dormant helpers or running watchers that can restore a stale worktree after an apparently successful deploy.
Every successful sync writes the status consumed by the live-source doctor.

liveChecks.description: 127.0.0.1 admin server root must match the checkout running this guard.
The stale July 6 worktree must never be the live admin root again.
Hermes/backend must run the same commit as the served checkout.

rules.title: Website Studio starts from the PhantomForce public site
rules.ownerDecision: Website Studio is for editing phantomforce.online first. It must not revert to a generic start screen or the old Termina store starter.

requiredPatterns.description: Blank Website Studio create opens the default public-site flow.
New blank site applies the PhantomForce public-site starter.
Empty UI says Edit the public site, not Start a website.
The template picker exposes the PhantomForce public-site starter.
The public-site starter is bound to phantomforce.online.

forbiddenPatterns.description: Rejected generic Website Studio headline.
Rejected generic Website Studio CTA.
Rejected store-starter framing.
Old Termina starter must not return as the default template.

forbiddenPatterns.pattern: Your terminal, organized\. Your prompts, on tap\.
forbiddenPatterns.description: Old Termina hero copy must not return.

rules.title: Accounting means confirmed transactions
rules.ownerDecision: Money became Accounting. It must track real/imported transaction records. Pipeline, won quotes, and retainers belong to goals or offer potential, not cash truth.

requiredPatterns.description: The nav label is Accounting, not Money.
Accounting surface has a transaction-reader section.
Cash collected is explicitly from real transactions.
Chat answers route explicit money questions to confirmed transactions in plain language.
Finance connector contract forbids counting pipeline as cash.

forbiddenPatterns.description: Rejected fake revenue-pipeline framing.
Rejected money-dashboard pipeline metric.

forbiddenPatterns.pattern: pipeline is either closed or waiting to be built
forbiddenPatterns.description: Rejected empty fake pipeline message.
Rejected internal accounting jargon on the customer-facing dashboard.
Explicit accounting answers use records and transactions, not ledger jargon.
Accounting empty state must say no transactions yet.

rules.title: Saved memory and temporary history stay separate
rules.ownerDecision: Do not save hellos or throwaway chatter to durable memory. Temporary chat history is lower-value context and shreds after 10 days.

requiredPatterns.description: Temporary chat history has a 10-day retention cap.
Trivial chatter is filtered out.
Temporary chat history is typed separately from saved memory.

requiredPatterns.pattern: Temporary chat history is separate and shredded after
requiredPatterns.description: Memory UI tells the truth about separation and shredding.

requiredPatterns.pattern: temporary history should stay within the context window cap
requiredPatterns.description: Retention tests cover temporary history limits.
Chat-only and do-not-save instructions can never promote temporary context into durable memory.

requiredPatterns.pattern: chat-only context must never be promoted to durable memory
requiredPatterns.description: Storage-level regression proves temporary context stays out of saved memory.

rules.title: Sidebar keeps structural utilities in the bottom zone
rules.ownerDecision: Settings, Developer, Away Mode, and Memory stay in the split bottom utility section. Customization must not flatten them back into the main nav.

requiredPatterns.description: Customization locks structural modules.
Customization preserves bottom nav zone.
Shell contains dedicated bottom nav.
Main shell renders bottom-zone items separately.

rules.title: Organization switches must change the data scope
rules.ownerDecision: PhantomForce and ChicagoShots are separate businesses. Switching orgs must switch the active org/session/tenant scope, and cross-org reads must be denied.

requiredPatterns.description: Browser org switch calls the backend.
Org switch uses the auth switch endpoint.

requiredPatterns.pattern: export const currentTenantId = \(\) => cleanTenantSegment\(ctx\.session\?\.orgId \|\| workspaceMeta\(currentWs\(\)\)\?\.id \|\| currentWs\(\)\);
requiredPatterns.description: Workspace storage derives a tenant id from the active authenticated org first, then the active local workspace.
Database auth tests cover denied cross-org switching.

requiredPatterns.pattern: Client ledger record must stay in client tenant
requiredPatterns.description: Memory/ledger tests verify tenant boundaries.
Publishing/run queue tests verify cross-org denial.

rules.title: Kingdom Breakers duel has one player castle and one bot castle
rules.ownerDecision: Duel vs bot is a simple two-castle artillery fight: player weapon on one side, bot weapon on the other, winner takes out the other Warden.

requiredPatterns.description: Duel mode builds its own two-castle level.
Shots target the opposite owner in duel mode.
Win/loss checks use each side's Warden.
Bot aims from its own weapon.
Server catalog points at the latest fixed Kingdom Breakers version.

forbiddenPatterns.description: Regression guard against checking only one side twice.

rules.title: Phantom Rumble starts at menu and keeps ledge recovery
rules.ownerDecision: Phantom Rumble must not open on the victory overlay, and fighters need real ledge-save recovery so the game feels playable instead of instantly punishing.

requiredPatterns.description: The menu reset hides the victory overlay and restores the setup screen.
Ledge-save recovery exists.
Fighter state tracks ledge recovery and cooldown.
Bots and humans can climb back from ledge hang.
Server catalog points at the fixed Phantom Rumble version.
Offline catalog cache-busts the fixed Phantom Rumble version.

rules.title: Media Lab keeps simple layer transform controls
rules.ownerDecision: Media Lab/editor layer controls must stay simple and useful: reset transform, center/fill, align selected layers, and distribute selected layers must remain available on desktop and mobile.

requiredPatterns.description: Shared composition helper can align one or more selected layers.
Shared composition helper can distribute three or more selected layers.
Layer inspector exposes one-click transform reset.
Layer actions expose a one-click Select all control for editable layers.
Select all excludes base, locked, and hidden layers.
Layer inspector exposes alignment controls.
Layer inspector exposes distribution controls.
Alignment controls wrap in the compact editor panel.
Media Lab editor tests cover alignment behavior.

rules.title: PhantomCut keeps per-clip video volume controls
rules.ownerDecision: The video editor must let users set volume per video clip, show non-default volume on timeline cards, and apply that volume during preview/export instead of only offering mute.

requiredPatterns.description: PhantomCut normalizes per-clip video volume.
New video clips default to unmuted full volume.
The clip inspector exposes a volume slider.
PhantomCut supports per-clip fade in/out alpha.
Playback and export sync apply per-clip volume and fade.
The clip inspector exposes fade in/out sliders.
Text overlays fade with the selected clip.

requiredPatterns.pattern: Volume slider must normalize and save clip volume
requiredPatterns.description: PhantomCut volume behavior is covered by tests.
The PhantomCut editor test is available as an npm script.

rules.title: Client setup belongs in Settings, not Clients
rules.ownerDecision: Organization setup, plan testing, tool connections, and access setup live under Settings. Clients remains for client/customer pipeline work only.

requiredPatterns.pattern: <button class="plan-inner" data-open-ws="settings">[\s\S]*No real work loaded yet\.
requiredPatterns.description: Empty setup CTA opens Settings.
Organization management stays under Settings.

requiredPatterns.pattern: The empty setup CTA must open Settings, not the client pipeline
requiredPatterns.description: Regression test protects the setup route.

forbiddenPatterns.pattern: <button class="plan-inner" data-open-ws="leads">[\s\S]*No real work loaded yet\.
forbiddenPatterns.description: Rejected setup CTA into Clients.
Rejected Client Setup framing inside Organization settings.

rules.title: Customer accounts can safely test public plan restrictions
rules.ownerDecision: Customer test accounts on app.phantomforce.online must be able to switch public tiers to verify limits, locks, and restrictions without exposing the internal admin plan or running billing.

requiredPatterns.pattern: export async function assignLocalCustomerPlan\(session: AccessSession, planKey: string\)
requiredPatterns.description: Local customer accounts can switch public test tiers.
Backend exposes the customer plan switch endpoint.
Settings owns plan testing.
Settings renders tier switch controls.
Local customer entitlements are read from the backend.
Static regression test covers backend/frontend/nav wiring.
Customer test mode exposes the actual Free, Pro, and Elite tiers, never the legacy Starter key.

requiredPatterns.pattern: seed starts on Free Preview[\s\S]*customer1 can switch to Pro[\s\S]*customer1 can switch to Elite[\s\S]*switch back to Free Preview
requiredPatterns.description: The executable local-customer test proves restrictions change immediately across all three public tiers.

forbiddenPatterns.description: Customer accounts must never default to the internal admin plan.
Local customer entitlements must not be dropped.

rules.title: Dashboard chat keeps the thread and uses the lightest capable brain
rules.ownerDecision: Normal questions and short follow-ups use the instant route; action-free comparisons, critiques, creative planning, and feedback use bounded local reasoning; scoped business advice uses a local advisory route with only relevant organization context. All three stay action-free, never trust a browser-supplied private provider, and never degrade into an unrelated status dump.

requiredPatterns.description: Temporary recent turns are exposed as a bounded, workspace-scoped context window.
The dashboard sends recent temporary context with the current request.
Action-free instant and reasoning chat skip organization pulse and opportunity lookups.
Provider failure returns a safe local answer without pretending a provider succeeded.

requiredPatterns.pattern: dashboard chat quality checks passed \(\$\{casualPrompts.length\} prompts\)
requiredPatterns.description: The full dashboard brain is exercised across more than twenty casual prompts.
Smart casual chat uses the quality-tested local 14B model instead of the failed CLI alias or weaker arithmetic lane.

requiredPatterns.pattern: Every authenticated user gets action-free chat, reasoning, and scoped advice
requiredPatterns.description: Customer browsers as well as admins can reach safe instant and reasoning conversation.
A host-backed model gate checks corrected-subject continuity, natural preferences, arithmetic, latency, and business leakage.
Deterministic micro-tools handle arithmetic chains and exact ordinal list selection instead of gambling on model output.
A self-starting authenticated HTTP gate exercises long conversation, topic switching, correction, arithmetic, exact output, and the bounded context rollover.
Harmless informational and reasoning questions do not inherit business context merely because they mention a website, image, bank, invoice, contract, or post.
The server independently validates instant, reasoning, and advisory conversation; only scoped advisory chat receives relevant business context.
The fast model keeps a small default response budget but expands it when the user explicitly asks for more words, steps, bullets, examples, or long-form detail.

requiredPatterns.pattern: Do not append a follow-up question unless missing information prevents a useful answer
requiredPatterns.description: Ordinary acknowledgments and direct answers do not end with a needless engagement question.

requiredPatterns.pattern: rapidFireVerified: true,[\s\S]*uncertaintyVerified: true,[\s\S]*empathyVerified: true,[\s\S]*structuredOutputVerified: true,[\s\S]*adaptiveLengthVerified: true
requiredPatterns.description: The authenticated real-model gate protects long mixed-topic threads, uncertainty, empathy, structured output, and adaptive answer length.

requiredPatterns.pattern: tool_id: "phantom-identity"[\s\S]*Phantom's fast conversation lane is currently \${safeModel}
requiredPatterns.description: Phantom identity and active fast-lane model disclosure come from route truth rather than model improvisation.
Explicit exact-word-count requests are corrected deterministically when the model miscounts.
Generic engagement questions are stripped from direct acknowledgments while user-requested questions remain intact.
The real-model gate protects branded identity, model truth, practical code help, multilingual continuity, and refusal to fabricate sources.

requiredPatterns.pattern: export const MAX_INSTANT_CONTEXT_CHARS = 4800;[\s\S]*for \(let index = activeTurns.length - 1; index >= 0 && remaining > 0; index -= 1\)
requiredPatterns.description: Instant context is packed newest-first inside a hard budget so old long replies cannot cut off the latest correction or final rules.
The authenticated model gate proves a newest correction survives oversized prior turns.
Direct harmless personality questions receive an immediate Phantom-specific answer instead of an AI disclaimer or an expensive model call.
Short name, city, word, color, dessert, and similar only-requests deterministically remove explanatory tails.
The authenticated real-model gate rejects a fabricated fact, protects replacement-only behavior, and proves a natural multi-turn taste conversation.
Browser routing requires live-data intent around market nouns instead of escalating every mention of current, latest, or stock.

requiredPatterns.pattern: what does electrical current mean[\s\S]*how do I make chicken stock[\s\S]*why do stock photos look fake[\s\S]*what is the current Bitcoin price
requiredPatterns.description: Server policy tests protect both casual lexical uses and genuinely live market requests.
The authenticated real-model gate proves electrical current and stock-photo questions remain fast, accurate, and free of business leakage.
Temporary history is included only for genuine follow-ups or topic overlap, and standalone prompts automatically start a clean topic segment.
Provider-outage replies retrieve the relevant recent topic, deterministic personality/tools, and output constraints instead of behaving like a separate chatbot.

requiredPatterns.pattern: OLLAMA_BASE_URL: "http://127\.0\.0\.1:9"[\s\S]*fallback\?\.all_failed, true[\s\S]*instant chat HTTP forced-fallback checks passed
requiredPatterns.description: An authenticated HTTP gate forces Ollama offline and proves useful topic-safe local degradation without business leakage.
Questions about approval, memory, pipeline, accounting, proposals, leads, automation, and similar concepts stay informational unless the user explicitly references private workspace state.
A failed instant, reasoning, or advisory browser request remains in the conversation fallback and cannot fall through into business command keyword cascades.

requiredPatterns.pattern: offlineGeneralQuestions[\s\S]*tell me about bank robberies in movies[\s\S]*explicitWorkspaceQuestion
requiredPatterns.description: Offline browser tests prove business-adjacent concept questions remain text-only while an explicit workspace-state request still reaches the business surface.

requiredPatterns.pattern: Do you approve of pineapple on pizza[\s\S]*What do monitor lizards eat[\s\S]*Create an automation to scan my site weekly[\s\S]*explicit durable memory must create one real memory record
requiredPatterns.description: Browser intent tests protect ordinary language from approval, reminder, automation, task, website, status, and memory keyword collisions while preserving explicit operations.
Saved-memory detection requires an explicit forward-looking instruction and cannot pin autobiographical uses of the word remember.
An explicit durable-memory command creates a real pinned record and confirms the fact that was saved.

requiredPatterns.pattern: write a four-line poem about automation[\s\S]*what do monitor lizards eat[\s\S]*make this sentence grammatical
requiredPatterns.description: Server instant-route policy stays aligned with browser semantic boundaries instead of blocking harmless creative and lexical prompts.
Explicit verified octopus facts use a curated stable answer, and code-only responses deterministically remove Markdown fences.
The authenticated model gate proves an accounting-to-penguin topic switch and its shorter follow-up never inherit stale business content.
Natural implicit follow-ups and named returns retrieve the relevant bounded thread instead of blindly using the newest unrelated topic.
The authenticated real-model gate proves a natural travel follow-up and a corrected named-topic return after several intervening subjects.
The server pins every validated action-free route to the local model even if an authenticated browser forges private model and provider fields.

requiredPatterns.pattern: const advisoryOnly = isAdvisoryChatRequest[\s\S]*if \(!advisoryOnly && MONEY_CONTEXT_TERMS[\s\S]*if \(!advisoryOnly && plan.length && PLAN_CONTEXT_TERMS
requiredPatterns.description: Scoped business advice can see the active organization while unrelated accounting totals and today-plan status remain excluded.
The authenticated real-model gate proves creative planning, scoped organization advice, and forged-private-provider resistance.

requiredPatterns.pattern: give me a practical three-step plan for my business[\s\S]*should be plan
requiredPatterns.description: Natural modifiers such as practical and three-step do not knock a planning request out of the advisory lane.
The organization switch keeps the selector and chat composer disabled until session, entitlements, and isolated chat UI have all crossed the same boundary.

forbiddenPatterns.description: Rejected generic fallback that replaced unrelated questions with business status.

rules.title: Dashboard stays focused and sidebar utilities stay split
rules.ownerDecision: The dashboard presents one real organization brief, one primary Phantom chat, and the proof rail. Duplicate quick actions and the redundant chat utility row stay removed, while structural utilities remain in a dedicated bottom sidebar zone.

requiredPatterns.description: Dashboard has one real business snapshot above chat.
Sidebar shell has a dedicated utility zone.
Bottom-zone items render separately from business modules.
Dashboard snapshot is derived from real local organization records.
Desktop dashboard chat is bounded to the available viewport row so the composer stays visible and only message history scrolls.

forbiddenPatterns.description: Duplicate dashboard quick-action panel must not return.
Redundant Memory, Prompt Library, and Map row must not return inside chat.

rules.title: Every browser context follows the active authenticated organization
rules.ownerDecision: A database-auth or customer session must scope memory, temporary history, request context, and visible chat to the active organization id. Switching businesses clears the visible transcript immediately, while switching back restores only that organization's stored context. A legacy local admin session may continue to use its local workspace id.

requiredPatterns.description: Database and customer browser storage use the active organization id instead of the legacy global workspace id.
A successful authenticated organization switch removes the previous organization's visible transcript before rerendering.

requiredPatterns.pattern: organization B must not see organization A durable memory[\s\S]*switching back must restore only organization A temporary context
requiredPatterns.description: Storage regression proves durable and temporary browser context remains separate in both switch directions.

requiredPatterns.pattern: organization B receives no A memory, history, request context, or visible chat[\s\S]*organization A returns without organization B contamination
requiredPatterns.description: The real database-login browser fixture proves two-way isolation for UI, storage, request packets, and model answers.

forbiddenPatterns.description: Rejected fallback that collapsed every database-auth organization into the global PhantomForce workspace.

rules.title: Business records obey the authorized organization
rules.ownerDecision: CRM, proposals, approvals, assets, accounting transactions, and connector state are separate for every organization. The server derives record workspace identity from the authorized tenant, rejects nonmember tenant ids with 403, and browser hydration cannot resurrect a server-backed row that is missing or deleted.

requiredPatterns.description: Database sessions treat an explicit tenant id as a membership authorization decision.
Proposal workspace identity comes from the authorized server tenant, not a client field.
Approval workspace identity comes from the authorized server tenant.
Active-organization server collections replace stale server-backed proposal and approval rows.
A permanent source/store regression covers two organizations and all corrected boundaries.

requiredPatterns.pattern: CRM, proposals, approvals, assets, accounting, and connector state remain organization-isolated[\s\S]*client-supplied foreign workspace labels are ignored by the server
requiredPatterns.description: The authenticated Chrome fixture proves distinct business records, forged labels, reload, and direct nonmember denial.

rules.title: Finished answers and account recovery never leave users waiting or trapped
rules.ownerDecision: A completed Phantom answer must become readable in under one second regardless of length. A delayed startup greeting must never overwrite a real answer or volunteer accounting, pipeline, or approval data. Customer account access uses two primary choices, contextual recovery links, one-time invitation links, a 2FA escape, duplicate-submit locking, and calm user-facing outage copy.

requiredPatterns.description: Long ambient speech reveals in bounded chunks, while completed command answers render immediately.
The first user action cancels the delayed startup greeting before it can overwrite a real answer.
A dashboard restored after workspace navigation binds its primary chat form before optional widgets can fail.
The account gate keeps sign-in/create primary and recovery secondary.
2FA can restart and account forms cannot double-submit.
Invitation links use the authoritative one-time server acceptance route.

requiredPatterns.pattern: Recovery actions must be contextual links[\s\S]*Invitation links must open a real one-time workspace acceptance flow
requiredPatterns.description: Permanent auth UI regression checks cover recovery, invitation, 2FA, and submission safety.

forbiddenPatterns.description: The old unbounded one-character reveal delay made finished answers look slow.

forbiddenPatterns.pattern: The account system is not reachable[.] Start the backend
forbiddenPatterns.description: Customers must never receive operator-only backend instructions.
The unsolicited greeting must never disclose accounting or approval metrics.

rules.title: Named earlier chat topics survive unrelated conversation without sending the whole history
rules.ownerDecision: Phantom must be able to return to a specifically named temporary topic after many unrelated turns and honor its latest correction. Vague transformations such as explain it carry only the immediately preceding turn so an older business subject cannot hijack them. Other requests may send up to six newest active-organization turns plus four relevant older thread turns, always capped at ten. Temporary chat still shreds after ten days and never becomes durable memory unless it independently meets the explicit memory rules.

requiredPatterns.description: The client context pack isolates vague immediate transformations while reserving bounded space for named older threads and nearby corrections.

requiredPatterns.pattern: a vague transformation follow-up must carry only the immediately preceding turn[\s\S]*an older business topic must not leak through generic routing words
requiredPatterns.description: A direct packet regression prevents explain-it follow-ups from reviving an older pipeline topic.
Every model route receives the request-aware bounded context packet.

requiredPatterns.pattern: named older topic must survive nine intervening subjects[\s\S]*named-topic retrieval must remain privacy bounded
requiredPatterns.description: Authenticated Chrome proves the earlier topic and correction survive beyond the old eight-turn window.

requiredPatterns.pattern: Back to Nova: what color is her raincoat[?] Color only[.][\s\S]*longDistanceTopicRevisitVerified: true
requiredPatterns.description: The deployed real-model gate permanently covers the long-distance named-topic return.
Exact recalled colors and codenames are extracted from the relevant corrected thread instead of guessed by the model.

forbiddenPatterns.description: Blindly truncating to eight newest turns discards a specifically named earlier thread.

rules.title: Phantom clarifies genuine ambiguity and repairs misunderstandings without changing the requested idea
rules.ownerDecision: When multiple named people make a pronoun genuinely ambiguous, Phantom asks exactly one concise question naming the candidates. When the user says an answer was misunderstood, Phantom follows the latest correction. Combining one answer's idea with another answer's tone must preserve the selected idea and borrow only style, never substitute the other content. None of these turns may introduce workspace status.

requiredPatterns.pattern: When the user says you misunderstood[\s\S]*preserve the requested source idea[\s\S]*Ask one concise clarification question only when multiple interpretations are genuinely plausible
requiredPatterns.description: The local-model context contract distinguishes correction, content source, style source, and true ambiguity.
Ambiguous person references produce one deterministic question naming the actual candidates.

requiredPatterns.pattern: Do you mean Dana or Priya[?][\s\S]*misunderstandingRepairVerified: true
requiredPatterns.description: The real-model gate covers both ambiguity and first-idea/second-tone repair.

requiredPatterns.pattern: function crossAnswerStyleRepairReply[\s\S]*now with a little more spark[\s\S]*crossAnswerStyleRepairReply[(]userRequest, turns[)]
requiredPatterns.description: Exact cross-answer playful repair cannot drift into another language, substitute content, or append model commentary.

requiredPatterns.pattern: ambiguous browser pronoun must produce one useful clarification naming both candidates
requiredPatterns.description: Authenticated Chrome proves the clarification appears through the real dashboard chat path.

rules.title: Phantom resolves exact object references, reorders real options, and retains chained corrections
rules.ownerDecision: Former and latter must resolve to values explicitly stated by the user, never a plausible invented noun. Numbered list movement or swapping must return the actual reordered options, never placeholders. Plural ownership must retain every named subject and item. Consecutive corrections must preserve the newest day, time, and location while excluding superseded values. All context remains temporary, organization-scoped, and bounded.

requiredPatterns.description: Exact former/latter and ordinal list operations run before generic model continuation.
The 100-plus request local-model gate covers exact references and correction chains.

requiredPatterns.pattern: latter must resolve to the second stated object value without invention[\s\S]*the browser must retain the latest value from every correction dimension
requiredPatterns.description: Authenticated Chrome proves the behavior through the real organization-scoped dashboard path.

rules.title: Phantom preserves explicit causes and rolls corrections back without reviving unrelated values
rules.ownerDecision: When multiple outcomes have different causes, Phantom must return the cause attached to the requested outcome and never invent a generic explanation or borrow another outcome's cause. A callback such as that reason must return the linked outcome. Explicit full rollback restores the original state; partial rollback restores only the named field and preserves every other accepted correction. All handling remains bounded, temporary, organization-scoped, and action-free.

requiredPatterns.description: Explicit ordinal causes and reason-to-outcome callbacks use bounded extraction instead of model invention.
The 100-plus request gate covers causal selection and both rollback forms.

requiredPatterns.pattern: browser must extract the cause attached to the requested ordinal result[\s\S]*a partial rollback must restore only the named field
requiredPatterns.description: Authenticated Chrome proves causal continuity and rollback through the real dashboard.

rules.title: Phantom preserves respectively mappings and rolls back only the named person's change
rules.ownerDecision: Respectively statements are exact ordered mappings: named, ordinal, and reverse callbacks must preserve the stated pairs. Unequal lists must produce one useful clarification instead of a guessed mapping. An undo naming one person restores only that person's original value while preserving every accepted change for the others. Handling remains bounded, extractive, temporary, and organization-scoped.

requiredPatterns.description: Ordered mappings, mismatched-list clarification, and per-person rollback use bounded deterministic state.
The 100-plus request gate covers forward, reverse, ordinal, unequal, and named rollback behavior.

requiredPatterns.pattern: reverse respectively callback must return the value's actual owner[\s\S]*undoing Mina must not roll Theo back with her
requiredPatterns.description: Authenticated Chrome proves the exact mappings and scoped undo through dashboard chat.

rules.title: Phantom compares corrected named values and follows explicit event order without invention
rules.ownerDecision: Named quantities keep their owners and units through winner, difference, and ranking questions. A later correction updates only that person's value. Ties are reported honestly; incompatible units, repeated event targets, and missing values receive one concise clarification instead of guessed arithmetic or event selection. Explicit numbered timelines return only the adjacent stated event. All context remains temporary, bounded, and organization-scoped.

requiredPatterns.pattern: function namedQuantityComparisonReply[\s\S]*Those values use different units[\s\S]*function orderedEventReferenceReply
requiredPatterns.description: Exact comparisons, scoped corrections, unit checks, and ordered-event callbacks use bounded deterministic state.
The 100-plus authenticated gate covers rankings, differences, corrections, ties, missing values, units, and event order.

requiredPatterns.pattern: equal values must report a tie instead of inventing a winner[\s\S]*a repeated event target must clarify the occurrence instead of choosing one
requiredPatterns.description: Authenticated Chrome proves comparison and timeline behavior through the real dashboard.

rules.title: Live admin and customer auth depend on a self-recovering database
rules.ownerDecision: admin.phantomforce.online uses the database-backed platform super-admin account, while app.phantomforce.online uses tenant-scoped customer accounts. Hermes must revive the approved local PostgreSQL container before booting so a Windows restart cannot silently fall back to incompatible customer-only auth.

requiredPatterns.description: Hermes verifies or starts local PostgreSQL before binding the API.
The approved database container survives normal host restarts.
Database auth advertises the real signup endpoint on the customer host.
Database auth advertises the real password-recovery endpoint.
